sub_mechanisms: M_1: assemble per-head importance scores into a Dirichlet series D(s) = Σ a_n / n^s with a_n = importance of head n
M_2: analytically continue D(s) past convergence region
M_3: evaluate continuation at s=0 (cumulative importance) and s=1 (boundary)
M_4: prune heads whose individual analytic-continuation contribution is below epsilon
stripped_llm_application: A Dirichlet-series-based head-pruning signal that treats per-head importance as series coefficients, analytically continues to special points, and uses the continuation values as a pruning criterion alternative to magnitude or attention-entropy methods.
novelty_claim_stripped: Magnitude pruning (Frankle-Carbin) and attention-entropy pruning use direct head statistics. The candidate uses the Dirichlet-continuation values as a derived signal, picking up global properties of the importance distribution.
